# Xquik

> X (Twitter) real-time data platform. Real-time account monitoring, data extraction, giveaway draws, and a developer API with REST endpoints, HMAC webhooks, and an MCP server for AI agents.

Xquik tracks tweets, quotes, replies, retweets, and follower changes from monitored X accounts and delivers events via REST API polling, HMAC-signed webhooks, or an MCP server (Model Context Protocol) for AI agent integration. The platform provides 20 extraction tools for pulling replies, reposts, quotes, followers, following, verified followers, community members, community moderators, community posts, community search, list members, list posts, list followers, mentions, posts, threads, articles, Space participants, and people search results from any public account, tweet, list, community, or Space. It also offers giveaway draws with configurable filters and X data lookups (tweets, users, follower checks).

## Authentication

Two methods:

1. **API Key**: Format `xq_` + 64 hex characters. Pass via `x-api-key` header. Generate keys from the dashboard or `POST /api/v1/api-keys`.
2. **OAuth 2.1 Authorization Code with PKCE**: For browser-based MCP clients (Claude.ai, ChatGPT Developer Mode). Discovery at `https://xquik.com/.well-known/oauth-authorization-server`. Dynamic client registration at `https://xquik.com/api/oauth/register`.

## Base URLs

- REST API: `https://xquik.com/api/v1`
- OpenAPI Spec: `https://xquik.com/openapi.json` (OpenAPI 3.1, RFC 9727 discovery)
- MCP Server: `https://xquik.com/mcp` (StreamableHTTP transport, API key or OAuth Bearer token)

## Rate Limits

- API endpoints (`/api/v1/*`): 10 req/s sustained, 20 req/s burst
- General requests: 60 req/s, 100 req/s burst
- Exceeding limits returns `429 Too Many Requests` with `Retry-After` header

## Error Format

All errors return JSON:

```json
{ "error": "error_code" }
```

Some errors include additional context:

```json
{ "error": "monitor_limit_reached", "limit": 1 }
```

HTTP status codes: `400` (validation), `401` (unauthenticated), `402` (no subscription / usage limit), `403` (limit reached), `404` (not found), `409` (conflict), `429` (rate limited), `500` (server error), `502` (temporarily unavailable).

Common error codes: `invalid_input`, `invalid_id`, `invalid_tweet_url`, `invalid_tweet_id`, `invalid_username`, `invalid_tool_type`, `invalid_format`, `invalid_params`, `missing_query`, `missing_params`, `webhook_inactive`, `api_key_limit_reached`, `unauthenticated`, `no_subscription`, `subscription_inactive`, `usage_limit_reached`, `no_addon`, `monitor_limit_reached`, `not_found`, `user_not_found`, `tweet_not_found`, `monitor_already_exists`, `x_api_rate_limited`, `internal_error`, `stream_registration_failed`, `x_api_unavailable`, `x_api_unauthorized`.

Retry strategy: retry only on `429` and `5xx`. Formula: `delay = baseDelay * 2^attempt + random(0, jitter)`. Max 3 retries. Respect `Retry-After` header.

## Pagination

Cursor-based. Responses include `hasMore` (boolean) and `nextCursor` (opaque string). Pass `nextCursor` as the `after` query parameter on the next request. Do not decode or construct cursors manually. Used for events, draws, extractions. Monitors, webhooks, and API keys return up to 200 items without pagination.

## Event Types (6)

- `follower.gained` - New follower gained
- `follower.lost` - Existing follower lost
- `tweet.new` - Original tweet posted
- `tweet.quote` - Quote tweet posted
- `tweet.reply` - Reply posted
- `tweet.retweet` - Retweet posted

## Giveaway Draw Filters

`POST /api/v1/draws` accepts `tweetUrl` (required) plus these optional filters:

- `winnerCount` (number) - Winners to select (default 1)
- `backupCount` (number) - Backup winners to select
- `uniqueAuthorsOnly` (boolean) - One entry per author
- `mustRetweet` (boolean) - Require retweet of the original tweet
- `mustFollowUsername` (string) - Username entrants must follow
- `filterMinFollowers` (number) - Minimum follower count
- `filterAccountAgeDays` (number) - Minimum account age in days
- `filterLanguage` (string) - Language code filter (e.g., `en`, `tr`, `es`)
- `requiredKeywords` (string[]) - Keywords that must appear in reply text
- `requiredHashtags` (string[]) - Hashtags required in reply (with `#` prefix)
- `requiredMentions` (string[]) - Usernames that must be mentioned (with `@` prefix)

Tweet author is auto-excluded. Filters run before author deduplication. Winners selected via cryptographically secure randomness.

## X Search Syntax

The `q` parameter in `GET /api/v1/x/tweets/search` supports X search operators:

- `from:username` - Tweets by a specific user
- `to:username` - Tweets replying to a user
- `#hashtag` - Tweets containing a hashtag
- `"exact phrase"` - Exact phrase match
- `OR` - Boolean OR between terms
- `-exclude` - Exclude tweets matching a term

## Export

Draws and extractions support export via `GET /api/v1/draws/:id/export` and `GET /api/v1/extractions/:id/export`. Formats: `csv`, `xlsx`, `md` (Markdown). Query parameter: `format`. Extraction exports have a 50,000 row limit.

## Webhook Delivery

- Signature: HMAC-SHA256. Header: `X-Xquik-Signature: sha256=HMAC_HEX_DIGEST`. Secret returned once at webhook creation - store securely.
- Headers: `Content-Type: application/json`, `X-Xquik-Signature`
- Requirements: HTTPS endpoint, must respond `2xx` within 10 seconds
- Retry: Exponential backoff (1s, 2s, 4s, 8s, 16s). 5 attempts max. After all fail: status `exhausted`.
- Delivery statuses: `pending`, `delivered`, `failed`, `exhausted`

Webhook payload structure:

```json
{
  "eventType": "tweet.new",
  "username": "elonmusk",
  "data": { "id": "1893456789012345678", "text": "...", "author": { "id": "44196397", "userName": "elonmusk", "name": "Elon Musk" }, "isRetweet": false, "isReply": false, "isQuote": false, "createdAt": "2026-02-24T14:22:00.000Z" }
}
```

The `data` field contains the raw tweet object from the X real-time stream. Fields vary by tweet type. Common fields: `id`, `text`, `author`, `isRetweet`, `isReply`, `isQuote`, `createdAt`. Reply tweets include `inReplyToId`. Quote tweets include `quoted_tweet`. Follower events (follower.gained, follower.lost) contain: followerId, followerUsername, followerName, followerFollowersCount, followerVerified.

## Tweet Composition Workflow

Three-step workflow for algorithm-optimized tweets via the `compose-tweet` MCP tool. Free, no usage cost:

1. `compose-tweet` with `step="compose"` - Start composing. Returns X algorithm engagement signals and follow-up questions.
2. `compose-tweet` with `step="refine"` - After answering follow-ups. Returns goal-specific tips, example patterns, media strategy, hashtag advice, CTA guidance.
3. `compose-tweet` with `step="score"` - Evaluate a draft against X algorithm ranking factors. Returns pass/fail checklist.

Goals: `engagement`, `followers`, `authority`, `conversation`.

## MCP Server

2 tools, code-execution sandbox model at `https://xquik.com/mcp`:
`explore` — search the API spec for endpoints (free, no network calls, runs against in-memory catalog). `xquik` — execute any API call by writing a short async function that runs in a sandboxed environment with an authenticated `fetch`. Covers all 81 REST endpoints across 11 categories (account, bot, composition, extraction, integrations, media, monitoring, trends, twitter, x-accounts, x-write).

## Radar

Trending topics and news aggregated from 7 sources. Free for all subscribers, no usage cost.

**Sources:** `github`, `google_trends`, `hacker_news`, `polymarket`, `reddit`, `trustmrr`, `wikipedia`

**Categories:** `general`, `tech`, `dev`, `science`, `culture`, `politics`, `business`, `entertainment`

**REST API:** `GET /api/v1/radar` with query parameters: `source`, `category`, `hours` (1-72, default 6), `limit` (1-100, default 50), `region` (US, GB, TR, ES, DE, FR, JP, IN, BR, CA, MX, global), `after` (cursor). Alternative URL: `GET /api/v1/trending/:source` with kebab-case slugs (`reddit`, `github`, `hacker-news`, `google-trends`, `wikipedia`, `startups`, `polymarket`).

**MCP:** Use the `xquik` tool with `xquik.request('/api/v1/radar', { query: { source, category, hours, limit, region } })`.

**Response:** `{ items: RadarItem[], hasMore: boolean, nextCursor?: string }`

**RadarItem fields:** `id`, `title`, `description?`, `url?`, `imageUrl?`, `source`, `sourceId`, `category`, `region`, `language`, `score` (0-10000), `metadata` (source-specific), `publishedAt`, `createdAt`.

**Source-specific metadata:**
- GitHub: `starsToday`, `language` (programming language)
- Hacker News: `points`, `numberComments`, `author`
- Reddit: `subreddit`, `author`
- Google Trends: `approxTraffic`
- Wikipedia: `views`
- Polymarket: `volume`, `liquidity`, `outcomes`
- TrustMRR: `mrr`, `growthPercent`, `last30Days`, `customers`, `activeSubscriptions`, `onSale`, `category`, `xHandle`

## Extraction Tool Types (20)

By target type:

**Tweet target** (`targetTweetId`): `reply_extractor`, `repost_extractor`, `quote_extractor`, `thread_extractor`, `article_extractor`

**Username target** (`targetUsername`): `follower_explorer`, `following_explorer`, `verified_follower_explorer`, `mention_extractor`, `post_extractor`

**Community target** (`targetCommunityId`): `community_extractor`, `community_moderator_explorer`, `community_post_extractor`, `community_search` (also requires `searchQuery`)

**List target** (`targetListId`): `list_member_extractor`, `list_post_extractor`, `list_follower_explorer`

**Space target** (`targetSpaceId`): `space_explorer`

**Search** (`searchQuery`): `people_search`, `tweet_search_extractor`

All extraction tools accept an optional `resultsLimit` parameter to cap the number of results. When set, the extraction stops early instead of fetching all available data. The estimate endpoint also accepts `resultsLimit` to preview capped costs.

Always call the `extractions` tool with `action="estimate"` before `action="run"` to check cost and feasibility. Requires active subscription.

## Trending Regions (12)

| WOEID | Region |
|-------|--------|
| 1 | Worldwide |
| 23424977 | United States |
| 23424975 | United Kingdom |
| 23424969 | Turkey |
| 23424950 | Spain |
| 23424829 | Germany |
| 23424819 | France |
| 23424856 | Japan |
| 23424848 | India |
| 23424768 | Brazil |
| 23424775 | Canada |
| 23424900 | Mexico |

Subscription required, metered.

## Docs

- [Introduction](https://docs.xquik.com/introduction): Platform overview, capabilities, and integration methods.
- [Quickstart](https://docs.xquik.com/quickstart): Get your API key and make your first request in 2 minutes.
- [API Overview](https://docs.xquik.com/api-reference/overview): REST API v1 base URL, authentication, rate limits, and conventions.
- [Authentication](https://docs.xquik.com/api-reference/authentication): API key format, dual auth endpoints, key management, and security best practices.

## API Reference - Account & Keys

- [Get Account](https://docs.xquik.com/api-reference/account/get): Retrieve account info, subscription status, and current period usage.
- [Update Account](https://docs.xquik.com/api-reference/account/update): Update account locale preference (session auth only).
- [Set X Identity](https://docs.xquik.com/api-reference/account/x-identity): Link your X username to your Xquik account for own-account detection.
- [Create API Key](https://docs.xquik.com/api-reference/api-keys/create): Generate a new API key for programmatic access (session auth only).
- [List API Keys](https://docs.xquik.com/api-reference/api-keys/list): Retrieve all API keys with status and usage timestamps (session auth only).
- [Revoke API Key](https://docs.xquik.com/api-reference/api-keys/revoke): Permanently deactivate an API key. Immediate and irreversible.
- [Subscribe](https://docs.xquik.com/api-reference/account/subscribe): Get a Stripe checkout URL (new subscribers) or billing portal URL (existing subscribers). Free.

## API Reference - Monitors

- [Create Monitor](https://docs.xquik.com/api-reference/monitors/create): Start monitoring an X account for real-time tweet events.
- [List Monitors](https://docs.xquik.com/api-reference/monitors/list): Retrieve all monitors for your account.
- [Get Monitor](https://docs.xquik.com/api-reference/monitors/get): Retrieve details for a specific monitor by ID.
- [Update Monitor](https://docs.xquik.com/api-reference/monitors/update): Change event types or toggle active status for a monitor.
- [Delete Monitor](https://docs.xquik.com/api-reference/monitors/delete): Remove a monitor and stop tracking the X account.

## API Reference - Events

- [List Events](https://docs.xquik.com/api-reference/events/list): Query events from monitored accounts with filtering and cursor-based pagination.
- [Get Event](https://docs.xquik.com/api-reference/events/get): Retrieve a single event by ID with full details.

## API Reference - Webhooks

- [Create Webhook](https://docs.xquik.com/api-reference/webhooks/create): Register an HTTPS endpoint to receive real-time event notifications.
- [List Webhooks](https://docs.xquik.com/api-reference/webhooks/list): Retrieve all webhook endpoints registered to your account.
- [Update Webhook](https://docs.xquik.com/api-reference/webhooks/update): Update the URL, event types, or active state of an existing webhook.
- [Delete Webhook](https://docs.xquik.com/api-reference/webhooks/delete): Deactivate a webhook endpoint and stop all future deliveries.
- [Test Webhook](https://docs.xquik.com/api-reference/webhooks/test): Send a test payload to verify a webhook endpoint. Free, no usage cost.
- [List Deliveries](https://docs.xquik.com/api-reference/webhooks/deliveries): View delivery attempts and their statuses for a specific webhook.

## API Reference - Draws

- [Create Draw](https://docs.xquik.com/api-reference/draws/create): Execute a giveaway draw on a tweet to select random winners.
- [List Draws](https://docs.xquik.com/api-reference/draws/list): Retrieve draw history with cursor-based pagination.
- [Get Draw](https://docs.xquik.com/api-reference/draws/get): Retrieve draw details including winners and tweet metadata.
- [Export Draw](https://docs.xquik.com/api-reference/draws/export): Export draw data as CSV, XLSX, or Markdown.

## API Reference - Extractions

- [Create Extraction](https://docs.xquik.com/api-reference/extractions/create): Run an extraction job using one of 20 tools (reply, repost, quote, follower, following, verified follower, community, community moderator, community post, community search, list member, list post, list follower, mention, people search, post, thread, article, Space, tweet search). Supports optional `resultsLimit` to cap results.
- [List Extractions](https://docs.xquik.com/api-reference/extractions/list): Retrieve extraction job history with filtering and cursor-based pagination.
- [Get Extraction](https://docs.xquik.com/api-reference/extractions/get): Retrieve extraction job details with paginated results.
- [Export Extraction](https://docs.xquik.com/api-reference/extractions/export): Export extraction results as CSV, XLSX, or Markdown. 50,000 row limit.
- [Estimate Extraction](https://docs.xquik.com/api-reference/extractions/estimate): Get a cost estimate before running an extraction job.

## API Reference - X API

- [Get Tweet](https://docs.xquik.com/api-reference/x/get-tweet): Look up a single tweet with author information and optional attached media. Metered.
- [Search Tweets](https://docs.xquik.com/api-reference/x/search-tweets): Search tweets by query string with optional attached media. Metered.
- [Get User](https://docs.xquik.com/api-reference/x/get-user): Look up an X user profile by username. Metered.
- [Check Follower](https://docs.xquik.com/api-reference/x/check-follower): Check follow relationship between two X users. Metered.
- [Download Media](https://docs.xquik.com/api-reference/x/download-media): Download media from tweets. Supports single tweet or bulk (up to 50). Returns gallery URL. Metered on first download, cached afterward.

## API Reference - Styles

- [Analyze Style](https://docs.xquik.com/api-reference/styles/analyze): Fetch and cache recent tweets for a username. Returns tweet texts, media, and timestamps. Metered.
- [List Styles](https://docs.xquik.com/api-reference/styles/list): List all cached tweet style profiles with summary info (username, tweet count, fetched date).
- [Get Style](https://docs.xquik.com/api-reference/styles/get): Retrieve a cached style profile with full tweet data for a username.
- [Delete Style](https://docs.xquik.com/api-reference/styles/delete): Remove a cached tweet style profile.
- [Save Style](https://docs.xquik.com/api-reference/styles/save): Save a custom style profile from tweet texts. Free, no usage cost.
- [Compare Styles](https://docs.xquik.com/api-reference/styles/compare): Compare two cached style profiles side by side.
- [Analyze Performance](https://docs.xquik.com/api-reference/styles/performance): Get engagement metrics (likes, retweets, replies, quotes, views, bookmarks) for cached tweets. Metered.

## API Reference - Drafts

- [Create Draft](https://docs.xquik.com/api-reference/drafts/create): Save a tweet draft with optional topic and goal.
- [List Drafts](https://docs.xquik.com/api-reference/drafts/list): Retrieve saved drafts with cursor-based pagination.
- [Get Draft](https://docs.xquik.com/api-reference/drafts/get): Retrieve a specific draft by ID.
- [Delete Draft](https://docs.xquik.com/api-reference/drafts/delete): Remove a saved tweet draft.

## API Reference - Trends

- [List Trends](https://docs.xquik.com/api-reference/trends/list): Get trending topics by region. Subscription required, metered. 12 regions.

## API Reference - Radar

- [List Radar Items](https://docs.xquik.com/api-reference/radar/list): Trending topics and news from 7 sources (Google Trends, Hacker News, Polymarket, TrustMRR, Wikipedia, GitHub Trending, Reddit). Filter by source, category, region, and time window. Free for all subscribers, no usage cost.

## API Reference - X Accounts

- [List X Accounts](https://docs.xquik.com/api-reference/x-accounts/list): List all connected X accounts. Free.
- [Connect X Account](https://docs.xquik.com/api-reference/x-accounts/connect): Connect a new X account with credentials and optional TOTP. Free.
- [Get X Account](https://docs.xquik.com/api-reference/x-accounts/get): Get details for a connected X account. Free.
- [Disconnect X Account](https://docs.xquik.com/api-reference/x-accounts/disconnect): Remove a connected X account. Free.
- [Re-authenticate X Account](https://docs.xquik.com/api-reference/x-accounts/reauth): Re-authenticate an expired X account session. Free.

## API Reference - X Write API

- [Create Tweet](https://docs.xquik.com/api-reference/x-write/create-tweet): Post a tweet from a connected X account. Supports replies, attachments, community posts, note tweets, and media. Metered.
- [Delete Tweet](https://docs.xquik.com/api-reference/x-write/delete-tweet): Delete a tweet by ID. Metered.
- [Like Tweet](https://docs.xquik.com/api-reference/x-write/like): Like a tweet. Metered.
- [Unlike Tweet](https://docs.xquik.com/api-reference/x-write/unlike): Remove a like from a tweet. Metered.
- [Retweet](https://docs.xquik.com/api-reference/x-write/retweet): Retweet a tweet. Metered.
- [Follow User](https://docs.xquik.com/api-reference/x-write/follow): Follow an X user. Metered.
- [Unfollow User](https://docs.xquik.com/api-reference/x-write/unfollow): Unfollow an X user. Metered.
- [Send DM](https://docs.xquik.com/api-reference/x-write/send-dm): Send a direct message to an X user. Metered.
- [Update Profile](https://docs.xquik.com/api-reference/x-write/update-profile): Update display name, bio, location, or website URL. Metered.
- [Update Avatar](https://docs.xquik.com/api-reference/x-write/update-avatar): Upload a new profile picture. Max 700KB, GIF/JPEG/PNG. Metered.
- [Update Banner](https://docs.xquik.com/api-reference/x-write/update-banner): Upload a new banner image. Max 2MB, GIF/JPEG/PNG. Metered.
- [Upload Media](https://docs.xquik.com/api-reference/x-write/upload-media): Upload media for use in tweets. Supports file upload (multipart/form-data) or URL-based upload (JSON body with `url` parameter). GIF/JPEG/PNG/MP4. Metered.
- [Create Community](https://docs.xquik.com/api-reference/x-write/create-community): Create a new X community. Metered.
- [Delete Community](https://docs.xquik.com/api-reference/x-write/delete-community): Delete an X community. Requires name confirmation. Metered.
- [Join Community](https://docs.xquik.com/api-reference/x-write/join-community): Join an X community. Metered.
- [Leave Community](https://docs.xquik.com/api-reference/x-write/leave-community): Leave an X community. Metered.

## API Reference - Integrations

- [Create Integration](https://docs.xquik.com/api-reference/integrations/create): Create a Telegram or notification integration for monitor events. Free.
- [List Integrations](https://docs.xquik.com/api-reference/integrations/list): List all integrations. Free.
- [Get Integration](https://docs.xquik.com/api-reference/integrations/get): Get integration details including filters and templates. Free.
- [Update Integration](https://docs.xquik.com/api-reference/integrations/update): Update name, event types, filters, or active status. Free.
- [Delete Integration](https://docs.xquik.com/api-reference/integrations/delete): Remove an integration. Free.
- [List Integration Deliveries](https://docs.xquik.com/api-reference/integrations/deliveries): View delivery history for an integration. Free.
- [Test Integration](https://docs.xquik.com/api-reference/integrations/test): Send a test notification to verify the integration. Free.

## Webhooks

- [Webhooks Overview](https://docs.xquik.com/webhooks/overview): Real-time event delivery to your HTTPS endpoints with HMAC-SHA256 signatures.
- [Signature Verification](https://docs.xquik.com/webhooks/verification): Verify HMAC-SHA256 signatures on incoming webhook deliveries.

## MCP Server

- [MCP Server Overview](https://docs.xquik.com/mcp/overview): Connect AI agents and IDEs (Claude Desktop, Claude Code, ChatGPT, Codex CLI, Cursor, VS Code, Windsurf, OpenCode) to Xquik via Model Context Protocol.
- [MCP Tools Reference](https://docs.xquik.com/mcp/tools): 2 tools (`explore` + `xquik` sandbox) covering 81 endpoints across 11 categories with input/output schemas and usage examples. Code-execution sandbox model where agents write async JavaScript functions.
- [Docs MCP Server](https://docs.xquik.com/mcp/docs-mcp): Search Xquik documentation from AI tools via a Mintlify-hosted MCP server at `https://docs.xquik.com/mcp`. No auth required. Separate from the API MCP server.

## OAuth

- [OAuth 2.1 (PKCE)](https://docs.xquik.com/oauth/overview): OAuth 2.1 Authorization Code flow with PKCE for MCP server authentication. Discovery endpoints, dynamic client registration, authorization, token exchange, refresh tokens.

## AI Agent Skill

- [Xquik Skill](https://github.com/Xquik-dev/x-twitter-scraper): AI agent skill that gives 40+ coding agents (Claude Code, Cursor, GitHub Copilot, Codex, Windsurf, VS Code, Gemini CLI, and more) deep knowledge of the Xquik API. Agents can write API integrations, set up webhook handlers with HMAC verification, configure MCP connections, and use the 2 MCP tools (explore + xquik sandbox) covering 81 endpoints and 20 extraction tool types with correct parameters. Install via `npx skills add Xquik-dev/x-twitter-scraper`.

## Guides

- [Workflows](https://docs.xquik.com/guides/workflows): End-to-end integration patterns with complete code examples.
- [Error Handling](https://docs.xquik.com/guides/error-handling): Error recovery patterns, retry strategies, and rate limit handling.
- [Rate Limits](https://docs.xquik.com/guides/rate-limits): Rate limit tiers, burst allowances, and client-side rate limiting.
- [Webhook Testing](https://docs.xquik.com/guides/webhook-testing): Test webhook integrations locally with ngrok, RequestBin, and CLI tools.
- [Billing & Usage](https://docs.xquik.com/guides/billing): Subscription pricing, usage quotas, and monthly operation limits.
- [Trends](https://docs.xquik.com/guides/trends): Trending topics across 12 regions, caching behavior, and workflow examples combining trends with tweet search.
- [Extraction Workflow](https://docs.xquik.com/guides/extraction-workflow): End-to-end extraction guide. Estimate costs, run jobs, paginate results, and export files. All 20 tool types with code examples in 4 languages.

## Resources

- [Platform Architecture](https://docs.xquik.com/guides/architecture): Architecture overview, security model (API key auth, data isolation, authorization), rate limits, usage limits, monitoring architecture, and platform limitations.
- [Troubleshooting](https://docs.xquik.com/guides/troubleshooting): Common issues and solutions for the Xquik API.
- [Type Definitions](https://docs.xquik.com/guides/types): TypeScript type definitions for all API request and response objects.
